question:
Solve the following fractions problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

A group of 30 tourists sets out on a walking tour of the rainforest. Two tourists are eaten by anacondas, then half the remaining tourists try to pet poisonous dart frogs and get poisoned. If only 1/7 of the poisoned tourists recovered, how many tourists are left at the end of the tour?
response:
Okay, so there are 30 tourists at the start. If 2 are eaten by anacondas, that leaves 28 tourists. Then, half of the remaining tourists get poisoned, so half of 28 is... umm, 14, I think? So 14 are poisoned.

Now, only 1/7 of the poisoned tourists recover. I think 1/7 of 14 is 2 (because 14 ÷ 7 = 2). So 2 recover, but the rest of the poisoned ones don’t... which means 14 - 2 = 12 are gone. 

So, 28 (after anacondas) - 12 (who didn’t recover) = 16 tourists left? I hope that’s right, but I’m not super sure.
